reforms.description: Iceland has significantly expanded its R&D tax incentive scheme, raising the annual ceiling on qualifying R&D expenditure and introducing differentiated tax credit rates for SMEs and large firms, while also introducing and widening several tax reliefs aimed at fostering innovation, including measures for green investment and charity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The expansion of R&D tax incentives is expected to enhance innovation and support small businesses, thereby raising productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant expansion of the R&D tax incentive scheme represents a major reform in the innovation policy landscape.
reforms.source_quote: A reform in 2020... raised the annual ceiling on qualifying R&D expenditure and introduced different tax credit rates for SMEs and large firms.
